meta.method: Public web research only (12+ web searches). Pricing listed only where published; third-party reported figures are labeled as such. Direct fetches of several vendor pricing pages were blocked (403), so some pricing relies on third-party review sites and is flagged.

competitors.name: HiJiffy
competitors.positioning: Guest communications hub / '#1 hotel chatbot' for direct bookings; AI concierge across WhatsApp, web, Instagram for hotels.
competitors.pricing: Not officially public on captured pages. Third-party reporting (hoteltechinsight.com, 2025) cites ~EUR 4/room/month Starter tier, ~EUR 120/mo for a 30-room boutique rising to EUR 200-300/mo with modules.
competitors.target: Independent hotels, boutique properties, hotel groups (Europe-heavy).
competitors.proof: Widely ranked in hotel chatbot roundups (HotelTechReport, Viqal, Conduit lists).
competitors.weaknesses: Per-room SaaS pricing assumes a PMS-integrated hotel; onboarding reported at 1-3 weeks and value depends on the hotel doing content/FAQ setup and staff adoption (upmarket.cloud implementation guide). ASSUMPTION: thin hands-on presence for small Bali villas/guesthouses without a PMS.

competitors.name: Asksuite
competitors.positioning: AI Reservation Assistant + omnichannel inbox for hotels; converts WhatsApp/web/Instagram inquiries into direct bookings with real-time quotes.
competitors.pricing: Not public (custom quotes). Third-party estimates ~$150-300/mo for small/mid independents (hoteltechinsight.com); dialzara.com cites 'starts at $199/mo'.
competitors.target: Independent hotels and resorts globally, strong in LatAm; 80+ countries.
competitors.proof: Site claims 5,500+ hotels, 147M travelers served, 2.1B messages, $586M direct revenue, HotelTechAwards #1 chatbot 7 years running (asksuite.com).
competitors.weaknesses: Booking-engine/PMS-centric; requires demo/sales cycle and onboarding. ASSUMPTION: overkill and under-localized for small Indonesian villa operators who live on WhatsApp without a booking engine.

competitors.name: Canary Technologies
competitors.positioning: Hotel guest management platform (check-in, upsells, AI guest messaging, AI voice) — 'AI that talks to hotel guests for you'.
competitors.pricing: Not public on site. Third-party roundups cite entry tiers around $99/mo scaling to custom enterprise (conduit.ai / viqal roundups).
competitors.target: Hotels from independents up to major chains (US-centric).
competitors.proof: Used by major hotel brands per its site and industry roundups.
competitors.weaknesses: Platform breadth means implementation and PMS integration work; sold via sales team. ASSUMPTION: no on-the-ground SEA implementation support for small properties.

competitors.name: Duve
competitors.positioning: Guest experience OS (online check-in, guest app, upsells, AI communication) for hotels and short-term rentals.
competitors.pricing: Partially public via comparison pages: ~$5/room/mo base rising to ~$9-10/room/mo with communication suite and upsells; Duve takes ~10% of upsell revenue (ensoconnect.com comparison, operto.com).
competitors.target: Hotels, aparthotels, vacation rental managers.
competitors.proof: Featured across hotel-tech comparison sites.
competitors.weaknesses: Per-room fees plus upsell rev-share; setup and content still on the operator. ASSUMPTION: limited Bahasa Indonesia/local OTA nuance for Bali operators.

competitors.name: HostBuddy AI
competitors.positioning: AI guest messaging co-host for short-term rentals; integrates with Hostaway, Guesty, OwnerRez, Hospitable etc.; claims 40-50% of messages on autopilot.
competitors.pricing: PUBLIC: Pro $7/property/mo (1-10 listings), $6 (11-50), $5 (51-99); Elite $10/$8/$6 per property/mo; 14-day free trial (hostbuddy.ai pricing + userguide).
competitors.target: Self-managing Airbnb hosts and STR property managers (US-centric).
competitors.proof: Positive reviews on BNBCalc, Capterra listings; PMS marketplace listings.
competitors.weaknesses: Requires a supported PMS (many Bali villa operators run on WhatsApp + spreadsheets, no PMS); the host must build/maintain the property knowledge base themselves; ~50% automation ceiling reported by users (bnbcalc.com review).

competitors.name: Besty AI
competitors.positioning: AI guest communication + upsell engine for vacation rental portfolios (Hostaway/Guesty ecosystem).
competitors.pricing: Not public. Third-party reporting: base subscription plus 10-15% revenue share on AI-generated upsells; custom quotes via demo (nowistay.com / stayfi roundups).
competitors.target: Professional STR property managers with 10+ listings.
competitors.proof: Claims ~30-40% of guest comms handled autonomously (third-party roundups); Hostaway marketplace partner.
competitors.weaknesses: PMS-dependent; rev-share model; sales-led onboarding. ASSUMPTION: not viable for non-PMS operators or mixed WhatsApp/OTA workflows common in Bali.

competitors.name: Enso Connect
competitors.positioning: AI-powered guest experience platform ('Boarding Pass' journey) for STRs and boutique hotels.
competitors.pricing: Not public (custom quote); charges 5% on upsells per its own comparison page.
competitors.target: STR property managers and boutique hotels (North America/Europe).
competitors.proof: HotelTechReport and Capterra presence; PMS marketplace integrations.
competitors.weaknesses: Quote-only pricing; platform depth requires real implementation effort by the operator. ASSUMPTION: no SEA presence.

competitors.name: Conduit (formerly HostAI)
competitors.positioning: Agentic conversational AI for hospitality — routes messages, assigns tasks, automates responses across teams; one-click install via Guesty/Hostaway marketplaces.
competitors.pricing: Not public.
competitors.target: STR managers and hotels already on major PMSs.
competitors.proof: Publishes its own tested rankings/content; marketplace listings.
competitors.weaknesses: PMS-gated; self-serve setup still assumes an ops-savvy buyer. ASSUMPTION: minimal support for Indonesian channels (WhatsApp-first, local OTAs like Traveloka/Tiket).

competitors.name: Structurely
competitors.positioning: Standalone AI ISA ('Aisa Holmes') qualifying real-estate leads via text, email, chat and voice; 13M+ conversations claimed.
competitors.pricing: Not public on captured pages (pricing page blocked). Third-party figures conflict: $179/mo starting (realestatetoolkit.ai, listingflare.com) vs newer reporting of $499/mo + $0.12 per action credit (nurtureos.io, 2026).
competitors.target: US real-estate agents and teams wanting an AI ISA without switching CRM/marketing stack.
competitors.proof: 13M+ conversations claim; long track record in AI-ISA category (listingflare).
competitors.weaknesses: Per-action credit costs (pay per SMS regardless of outcome, per nurtureos.io); English/US-market scripts. ASSUMPTION: not localized for SEA markets or WhatsApp-first lead flows.

competitors.name: Ylopo (raiya AI)
competitors.positioning: Full real-estate digital marketing + AI ecosystem; raiya AI does ChatGPT-powered texting plus AI voice calls and live transfers for lead follow-up.
competitors.pricing: Not public (demo required). Third-party estimates: platform ~$295-500/mo, setup fee $1,000-2,000, realistic all-in $600-800/mo minimum and $1,500-3,500+/mo for teams including ad spend (luxurypresence.com, inboundrem.com, theclose.com).
competitors.target: US real-estate teams and brokerages with ad budgets.
competitors.proof: Category leader in AI lead follow-up reviews (swiftleadsai comparison, theclose).
competitors.weaknesses: Requires buying the whole Ylopo ecosystem + ad spend; not a la carte (swiftleadsai.com comparison). US-only focus.

competitors.name: Verse.ai (formerly Agentology)
competitors.positioning: Human + AI hybrid lead conversion concierge (24/7 SMS-first outreach and qualification) — effectively an outsourced ISA service, not just software.
competitors.pricing: Not public ('does not list pricing on its site at all' — hooquest). Third-party: ~$1,800+/mo enterprise-level concierge (itqlick.com, hooquest.com).
competitors.target: Real-estate teams, brokerages, and other lead-heavy SMBs (US).
competitors.proof: Long-running category presence; hooquest ISA rankings.
competitors.weaknesses: Priced for US enterprise teams; opaque pricing; service layer proves demand for done-for-you but at recurring cost far above a one-time $5-10k build.

competitors.name: Conversion Monster
competitors.positioning: Human ISA service that calls/nurtures/qualifies real-estate leads on the agent's behalf.
competitors.pricing: Third-party reported: from ~$795+/mo (hooquest ISA roundup). Not confirmed on vendor site in this research.
competitors.target: US agents/teams with inbound lead flow.
competitors.proof: Listed among best ISA companies (hooquest).
competitors.weaknesses: Pure human service — recurring $9.5k+/yr cost; benchmark showing businesses already pay service prices for outcomes SaaS alone doesn't deliver.

competitors.name: Smith.ai
competitors.positioning: 24/7 virtual receptionists (human) + AI Receptionist product; answering, intake, scheduling for professional services incl. medical/dental-adjacent SMBs.
competitors.pricing: PUBLIC: human receptionist plans ~$292.50/mo for 30 calls (~$9.75/call), $765/mo for 90 calls, $1,950/mo for 300 calls; AI Receptionist from $95/mo (~60 calls) with $1.60-$2.40 per-call rates (smith.ai pricing pages; corroborated by schedulingkit.com, loman.ai).
competitors.target: US law firms, home services, medical/dental offices, SMBs.
competitors.proof: Large review footprint; published per-call model.
competitors.weaknesses: Per-call costs scale painfully with volume ($9.75+/call human); generic scripts, not deep integration into a clinic's PMS/EHR or a hotel's stack; US business hours/accents focus.

competitors.name: Weave
competitors.positioning: All-in-one phone + patient communication platform (VoIP, texting, reminders, reviews, payments, AI assistant) for dental, optometry, vet, medical practices.
competitors.pricing: Partially public: entry ~$199-249/mo per office (getweave.com pricing page & costbench), realistic $249-350+/mo per third parties, plus one-time $750 setup fee and $200 forms-upload fee (emitrr.com, themolarreport.com).
competitors.target: US small-to-mid healthcare practices.
competitors.proof: Publicly traded (NYSE: WEAV); large install base.
competitors.weaknesses: Setup fees and hidden add-on costs are a documented complaint (emitrr, quo.com); US-only telephony; still a toolbox the practice must configure and staff must adopt.

competitors.name: Solutionreach
competitors.positioning: Patient engagement/communication platform (reminders, recall, messaging) for dental/medical.
competitors.pricing: Partially public: starts ~$329/mo; third parties report realistic $400-800/mo for small practices, $1,500+/mo for larger orgs (softwarefinder.com, itqlick.com).
competitors.target: US dental and medical practices.
competitors.proof: Long-established category incumbent.
competitors.weaknesses: Legacy platform; contract + custom-quote model; configuration burden on practice staff (per review-site complaints).

competitors.name: Klara (ModMed)
competitors.positioning: HIPAA-compliant patient messaging and communication platform, now part of ModMed.
competitors.pricing: Not public — demo/sales required (emitrr.com, capterra); some directories cite ~$12/user/mo base but this is unverified and likely not reflective of deployment cost.
competitors.target: US medical practices, esp. dermatology/specialty (ModMed EHR base).
competitors.proof: ModMed acquisition; established brand.
competitors.weaknesses: Opaque pricing; tied to US EHR ecosystem; irrelevant to non-US clinics without those systems.

competitors.name: Freed Front Desk
competitors.positioning: AI receptionist that answers clinic calls 24/7 (launched Apr 2026 by AI-scribe company Freed); 'no IT setup required'.
competitors.pricing: PUBLIC: Starter $149/mo per block of 250 calls (getfreed.ai; Businesswire launch release).
competitors.target: US small clinics already using Freed's AI scribe.
competitors.proof: Freed's existing clinician user base; press coverage.
competitors.weaknesses: New product (2026); phone-only; self-serve — clinic still owns call-flow design, EHR/scheduling integration gaps.

competitors.name: Assort Health
competitors.positioning: Enterprise AI voice agents for healthcare call centers; claims 190M+ patient interactions.
competitors.pricing: Not public. Third-party: ~$1,500/mo starting, to $10,000+/mo for larger groups; quote-only (flexbone.ai comparison).
competitors.target: US multi-site specialty groups and health systems.
competitors.proof: 190M+ patient interactions claim; enterprise logos.
competitors.weaknesses: Enterprise sales motion and price point; inaccessible to small clinics — leaves the SMB clinic segment to cheap self-serve tools or nothing.

competitors.name: Arini
competitors.positioning: 'The leading AI receptionist for dentists' — AI phone scheduling for dental practices and DSOs.
competitors.pricing: Not public.
competitors.target: US/Canada dental groups and DSOs.
competitors.proof: YC-backed; dental-vertical focus cited in AI receptionist roundups (theaiagentreport.com).
competitors.weaknesses: Dental-only, US-only, phone-only. ASSUMPTION: no SEA/Indonesia capability.

competitors.name: Kata.ai
competitors.positioning: Indonesia's flagship conversational AI company — enterprise Indonesian-language NLP chatbots/WhatsApp for telcos, banks, FMCG.
competitors.pricing: Not public; described as 'premium for SMEs' in Indonesian chatbot comparisons (udeskglobal).
competitors.target: Indonesian enterprises (Telkomsel-class), not SMBs.
competitors.proof: Best-in-class Bahasa Indonesia NLP reputation (udeskglobal comparison).
competitors.weaknesses: Enterprise pricing and sales cycle put it out of reach of Bali SMBs — leaves the villa/clinic/agency segment unserved.

competitors.name: Mekari Qontak
competitors.positioning: Indonesian omnichannel CRM + WhatsApp Business API + AI chatbot platform (part of Mekari SaaS group).
competitors.pricing: PUBLIC: from ~IDR 400,000/month (~USD 25) plus Meta WhatsApp conversation fees (e.g., marketing conversation ~Rp 596/message) (qontak.com pricing; mekari.com WA API price guide).
competitors.target: Indonesian SMEs and mid-market across industries.
competitors.proof: Part of Mekari, one of Indonesia's largest B2B SaaS companies.
competitors.weaknesses: Horizontal tool, not a vertical solution: an SMB still has to design flows, write content, integrate calendars/PMS, and maintain it — no done-for-you implementation for hospitality/clinics. Bahasa-first UX may not fit expat-owned Bali businesses.

competitors.name: Qiscus
competitors.positioning: Indonesian omnichannel/WhatsApp API and chat SDK provider, developer-friendly, AI-powered automation.
competitors.pricing: Not public — sales contact required (ycloud.com provider roundup).
competitors.target: Indonesian businesses needing custom chat infrastructure; developer teams.
competitors.proof: Established Meta BSP in Indonesia.
competitors.weaknesses: Developer-oriented — assumes the customer has engineers; SMB without tech staff can't self-implement.

competitors.name: WIZ.AI
competitors.positioning: Singapore-based enterprise voice AI ('talkbots') for SEA; Bahasa Indonesia incl. Javanese/Sundanese support; banking, telco, healthcare enterprise deployments.
competitors.pricing: Not public.
competitors.target: SEA enterprises and government; 30+ large clients.
competitors.proof: Series B funding led by SMBC Asia Rising Fund (prnewswire.com); localized SEA language stack.
competitors.weaknesses: Enterprise-only motion; a Bali villa or clinic cannot buy WIZ.AI — confirms the SEA SMB gap.

competitors.name: AI Avenue Bali
competitors.positioning: Self-described 'Bali's Leading AI Automation Agency' — tailor-made AI solutions for hospitality, real estate, retail, agencies, personal brands.
competitors.pricing: Not public (site fetch blocked; no published packages found via search).
competitors.target: Bali businesses across hospitality/real estate/retail.
competitors.proof: Not verified — no public case studies surfaced in search. ASSUMPTION: early-stage/boutique operation.
competitors.weaknesses: ASSUMPTION: low proof/case-study visibility; generic multi-vertical positioning rather than productized offers. Direct positional competitor to Selena Systems.

competitors.name: AI Consulting Bali
competitors.positioning: Bali AI consultancy publishing hospitality-focused content (hotel chatbots, resort automation, dynamic pricing for villas/resorts/boutique hotels).
competitors.pricing: Not public.
competitors.target: Bali villas, resorts, boutique hotels.
competitors.proof: Content marketing citing generic industry stats; no named client case studies found in search. ASSUMPTION: SEO-content-led, thin delivery evidence.
competitors.weaknesses: ASSUMPTION: appears content-site-first; no published pricing, packages, or verifiable client proof. Direct positional competitor in Bali hospitality AI.

competitors.name: H-Studio
competitors.positioning: Bali/Indonesia digital studio: websites, villa/hotel booking systems, WhatsApp automation, CRM integrations, AI chatbots, custom software.
competitors.pricing: Not public.
competitors.target: Bali/Indonesia villas, hotels, local businesses.
competitors.proof: Established web/booking-system portfolio per its site. ASSUMPTION: AI is an add-on service line, not core competency.
competitors.weaknesses: ASSUMPTION: web-dev DNA — AI/automation likely shallower than a dedicated AI implementer; closest local competitor for done-for-you WhatsApp automation.

competitors.name: Konten.ai
competitors.positioning: Done-for-you AI content agency (Gianyar, Bali) — sets up and manages AI agents that auto-generate and post social content for villas, hotels, guesthouses, SMEs.
competitors.pricing: PUBLIC (per site copy surfaced in search): from Rp 500K/month (~USD 30/mo).
competitors.target: Bali villas, hotels, guesthouses, SMEs.
competitors.proof: Not verified beyond own site.
competitors.weaknesses: Content/social-only — does not touch guest messaging, bookings, lead follow-up, or operations; price point signals a low-ticket productized service, different tier than $5-10k implementations.

competitors.name: CallSphere
competitors.positioning: AI voice/call agent vendor publishing SEO content targeting Bali vacation rental management with AI agents.
competitors.pricing: Not public in sources reviewed.
competitors.target: Vacation rental operators (content targets Bali explicitly).
competitors.proof: Not verified; blog content only. ASSUMPTION: remote SaaS vendor using Bali as an SEO keyword, no local presence.
competitors.weaknesses: ASSUMPTION: no on-the-ground implementation, no PMS/WhatsApp integration service — sells software, not outcomes.

saas_vs_service_gap: The pattern across all four verticals: self-serve SaaS is cheap ($5-10/property/mo HostBuddy, $99-300/mo hotel chatbots, $149/mo Freed, IDR 400k/mo Qontak) but it sells a tool, not an outcome — and the tool's value is gated on work the SMB owner can't or won't do. Concretely: (1) Integration prerequisites — nearly every hospitality AI assumes a PMS (Hostaway/Guesty/Cloudbeds) and every US clinic tool assumes a US EHR/phone stack; many Bali villas, agencies and clinics run on WhatsApp + spreadsheets + local OTAs, so the SaaS literally cannot connect. (2) Setup and knowledge-base burden — implementation guides admit 2-4 months for advanced setups and 1-3 week vendor onboarding (upmarket.cloud), automation ceilings of 30-50% when hosts self-configure (Besty/HostBuddy user reports), and only 23% of hotel guests satisfied with chatbot interactions largely because bots aren't wired into reservation data (vertize.io). (3) Staff adoption and maintenance — tools fail when nobody trains staff, monitors edge cases, or updates content (vertize.io, EHL insights). (4) The high end proves buyers pay service prices for outcomes: Verse.ai ~$1,800/mo, Conversion Monster $795/mo, Smith.ai $9.75/call, Assort Health $1,500-10,000/mo, Weave's own $750 setup fee — but these are US-centric, recurring, and enterprise-gated. A one-time $5-10k done-for-you implementation wins exactly where these two curves miss: the buyer wants the Verse/Assort outcome (someone else makes it work end-to-end, integrated with their actual messy stack, staff trained, maintained) at a total cost far below $20k+/yr recurring service fees — and in Bali/SEA specifically, where enterprise vendors won't go, US SaaS doesn't localize (Bahasa, WhatsApp-first, Traveloka/local workflows), and local players are either enterprise-only (Kata.ai, WIZ.AI), horizontal tools (Qontak, Qiscus), or thin boutique agencies with no published proof (AI Avenue Bali, AI Consulting Bali).
gaps: Identified gaps Selena Systems can own: (1) Bali/SEA SMB vertical implementations — no credible player does done-for-you AI for villas/guesthouses without a PMS, WhatsApp-first, multilingual (English + Bahasa + guest languages); local 'AI agencies' have no visible case studies or pricing, so the first firm to publish proof and productized $5-10k packages wins by default. (2) The 'no-PMS' hospitality segment — every guest-messaging SaaS assumes Hostaway/Guesty; a service that builds the messaging layer directly on WhatsApp Business API + Google Sheets/Airtable/simple booking stacks serves the majority of small Bali operators the SaaS ignores. (3) Real-estate AI ISA outside the US — Structurely/Ylopo/Verse are US-only and English-only; Bali/SEA property developers and villa sales agencies (high-ticket, WhatsApp leads, long nurture) have zero vertical options. (4) Clinic front-desk for medical-tourism/expat clinics in Bali — US tools (Weave, Klara, Freed) are locked to US telephony/EHRs; Assort-class voice AI is enterprise-only; aesthetic/dental/wellness clinics serving foreign patients need multilingual WhatsApp+voice intake nobody sells locally. (5) Fixed-price outcome packaging — competitors are either opaque quote-only (Asksuite, Enso, Verse, Kata.ai) or per-unit SaaS; publishing transparent $5-10k fixed-scope packages with an optional care/retainer plan differentiates on trust. (6) Maintenance retainer upsell — documented SaaS failure mode is post-launch drift (stale knowledge bases, unmonitored edge cases); a monthly optimization retainer converts one-time projects into recurring revenue that undercuts $795-1,800/mo US services. ASSUMPTION labels above mark inferences; all pricing claims carry source URLs or 'not public'.
